run.timeframe_label: 72h (strict window maintained — 8 verified items above expansion threshold)

items.title: Anthropic ships enterprise-managed MCP connector auth via Okta
items.summary: Anthropic launched enterprise-managed authorization for MCP connectors in beta on June 18, 2026, with Okta as the first supported identity provider. Admins authorize connectors once; users inherit access via IdP groups with zero-touch setup. Seven MCP providers at launch: Asana, Atlassian, Canva, Figma, Granola, Linear, Supabase. Beta for Team and Enterprise plans across Claude chat, Code, and Cowork.
key_claims: 7 MCP providers at launch (Asana, Atlassian, Canva, Figma, Granola, Linear, Supabase)
Okta as initial IdP; additional providers coming
Beta: Team and Enterprise plans
Optional enforcement: IdP-only connector access to prevent personal-account mixing
caveats: Beta status — onboarding automation experimental, subject to Okta API constraints
Slack and additional IdPs have no confirmed dates

items.title: Cursor 3.8 ships Automations platform with Slack triggers and GitHub event hooks
items.summary: Cursor 3.8 adds an Automations platform layer: /automate command for plain-language automation creation, Slack emoji trigger for agent dispatch, 5 new GitHub event hooks (issue comments, PR review comments, PR review submissions, review thread updates, workflow completion), and computer-use for producing demo screenshots/recordings in cloud automations.
key_claims: /automate command for plain-language automation creation
Slack emoji trigger: react to message to dispatch cloud agent
5 new GitHub event triggers
Computer use available in cloud automations for demo generation
caveats: GitHub-only version control integration; GitLab not supported
Slack notification customization limited — no per-event filtering at launch

items.title: Claude Code Artifacts publishes live shareable pages from agent sessions
items.summary: Claude Code Artifacts (beta, June 18, 2026) generates live versioned web pages from session full context (codebase, connectors, conversation) without separate data wiring. Pages auto-refresh on publish, maintain version history, are org-private by default. Use cases: incident pages, PR walkthroughs, license audits, cost dashboards, release checklists. Team and Enterprise only.
key_claims: Auto-refresh on publish; version history with restore
Org-only visibility — no public sharing
Built from session codebase + MCP connector data + conversation
Beta: Team and Enterprise plans only

items.title: Cursor 3.7 adds cloud subagents and reusable VM snapshots
items.summary: Cursor 3.7 (June 17) adds /in-cloud to spin up isolated VM subagents, reusable environment snapshots (target setup under 10 minutes on reuse), /babysit for autonomous PR preparation in cloud, and local-to-cloud context handoff. Cloud environment setup visible in shared terminal.
key_claims: Cloud environment setup under 10 minutes with snapshots
/babysit command for autonomous PR preparation
Reusable environment snapshots for faster future startups
Local-to-cloud session handoff without context loss

items.title: Anthropic ships Workload Identity Federation for keyless Claude API auth
items.summary: Workload Identity Federation (WIF) reached GA on Claude Platform June 17, 2026. Replaces static API keys with short-lived, scoped OIDC tokens. Compatible with AWS IAM, Google Cloud, or any OIDC issuer. JWT exchanged at POST /v1/oauth/token for Claude access token; SDK auto-refreshes. Service accounts GA; covers all Claude API endpoints, Python/TypeScript SDKs, Claude Code.
key_claims: Compatible with all OIDC providers (AWS IAM, Google Cloud, custom)
Short-lived token exchange at POST /v1/oauth/token; SDK auto-refresh
Covers all Claude API endpoints and Claude Code

items.title: Claude Design adds design-system imports and bidirectional Claude Code sync
items.summary: Claude Design update (June 17, 2026) adds design-system imports from GitHub, files, or raw uploads with automatic compliance checking; bidirectional /design-sync and /design commands between Design and Claude Code; richer canvas editor with layout controls; 6 new export connectors (Adobe, Base44, Gamma, Lovable, Miro, Replit). Shared usage limits across Claude products. Beta on Pro, Max, Team, Enterprise.
key_claims: Design-system imports from GitHub, files, or raw uploads
Bidirectional /design-sync and /design commands with Claude Code
6 new export connectors (Adobe, Base44, Gamma, Lovable, Miro, Replit)
Shared usage limits across Claude chat, Cowork, and Code

items.title: Databricks expands Agent Bricks with MCP, Grok, and Sandbox at Data+AI Summit
items.summary: At Data+AI Summit 2026 (June 15-18), Databricks announced: MCP support in Unity Catalog; Databricks Sandbox for isolated VM execution with scoped data access; Omnigent meta-harness for running LangGraph/CrewAI/OpenAI Agent SDK side-by-side; Document Intelligence SQL functions (ai_parse_document, ai_extract, ai_classify) GA; native Grok 4.3 integration via SpaceX partnership (confirmed June 18). Vendor reports 100,000+ agents built since launch.
key_claims: MCP support in Unity Catalog with existing data governance
Document Intelligence functions (ai_parse_document, ai_extract, ai_classify) now GA
100,000+ agents built since Agent Bricks launch (vendor metric)
Native Grok 4.3 integration confirmed June 18 via SpaceX partnership
caveats: 100,000 agents figure is vendor-reported, no methodology
Omnigent cross-framework compatibility may not cover all SDK versions

items.title: Z.ai releases GLM-5.2 open weights with MIT license and 1M-token context
items.summary: Z.ai (formerly Zhipu AI) released MIT-licensed weights of GLM-5.2 to HuggingFace and ModelScope on June 16, 2026. 753B-parameter mixture-of-experts model targeting long-horizon coding tasks. 1M-token context window. No regional restrictions for local deployment. Cloud API subscription from ~$12.60/month. Release timed to coincide with Fable 5/Mythos export restrictions. Community Code Arena ranking: second globally on long-horizon coding (T3 — no primary benchmark report at launch).
key_claims: 753B parameters, MIT license, HuggingFace and ModelScope (T2-T3 aggregated secondary)
1M token context window (T3 secondary)
~$12.60/month cloud API entry tier (T3 secondary)
Code Arena second globally on long-horizon coding (T3 community leaderboard)
caveats: API use subject to China's National Intelligence Law
US House May 2026 inquiry named Zhipu AI in Chinese AI cybersecurity scrutiny
No primary benchmark report published at launch
No native IDE integration — requires Cursor, Continue.dev plugins
Primary HuggingFace page not directly fetched; release facts from aggregated T2-T3 secondary sources
